verses.text: Tertullus accuses Paul before Felix, flattering the governor. Smooth flattery. The orator opens with empty praise.
Tertullus accuses Paul before Felix, flattering the governor. Smooth flattery. The orator opens with empty praise.
Tertullus accuses Paul before Felix, flattering the governor. Smooth flattery. The orator opens with empty praise.
Tertullus accuses Paul before Felix, flattering the governor. Smooth flattery. The orator opens with empty praise.
He calls Paul 'a pestilent fellow,' a mover of sedition, a ringleader of the Nazarenes. Heaped charges. They paint the apostle as a troubler.
He calls Paul 'a pestilent fellow,' a mover of sedition, a ringleader of the Nazarenes. Heaped charges. They paint the apostle as a troubler.
He calls Paul 'a pestilent fellow,' a mover of sedition, a ringleader of the Nazarenes. Heaped charges. They paint the apostle as a troubler.
He calls Paul 'a pestilent fellow,' a mover of sedition, a ringleader of the Nazarenes. Heaped charges. They paint the apostle as a troubler.
He calls Paul 'a pestilent fellow,' a mover of sedition, a ringleader of the Nazarenes. Heaped charges. They paint the apostle as a troubler.
Paul answers that they cannot prove their charges; he came to worship, not to stir strife. Plain truth. He meets slander with simple fact.
Paul answers that they cannot prove their charges; he came to worship, not to stir strife. Plain truth. He meets slander with simple fact.
Paul answers that they cannot prove their charges; he came to worship, not to stir strife. Plain truth. He meets slander with simple fact.
Paul answers that they cannot prove their charges; he came to worship, not to stir strife. Plain truth. He meets slander with simple fact.
'After the way which they call heresy, so worship I,' believing the law and prophets. Clear confession. He owns his faith without shame.
'After the way which they call heresy, so worship I,' believing the law and prophets. Clear confession. He owns his faith without shame.
'After the way which they call heresy, so worship I,' believing the law and prophets. Clear confession. He owns his faith without shame.
I came to bring alms; they found me purified, not in tumult; except for the resurrection. The real issue. The hope of rising is the true charge.
I came to bring alms; they found me purified, not in tumult; except for the resurrection. The real issue. The hope of rising is the true charge.

I came to bring alms; they found me purified, not in tumult; except for the resurrection. The real issue. The hope of rising is the true charge.
Felix defers them, keeping Paul with liberty. Delay. The governor puts off a hard decision.
Felix defers them, keeping Paul with liberty. Delay. The governor puts off a hard decision.
Paul reasons of righteousness and judgment; Felix trembles, 'Go thy way for this time.' A trembling sinner. He feels the truth yet puts it off.
Paul reasons of righteousness and judgment; Felix trembles, 'Go thy way for this time.' A trembling sinner. He feels the truth yet puts it off.
Hoping for a bribe, Felix leaves Paul bound to please the Jews. Greed and cowardice. Self-interest keeps an innocent man in chains.
